_comment: Category metadata for the Conscious Futures resource library -- color + one-line blurb per category, matching the cluster-color system from the design prototype. Kate can edit color/blurb here without touching site code. Order here also sets category-tile display order.

categories.name: Systems Change, Design & Futures Thinking
categories.blurb: How we imagine, forecast, and design for what's next -- and the frameworks for making change actually happen.

categories.name: Theories of Change & Impact Mapping
categories.blurb: Frameworks and tools for how change actually happens, and how to know if it did.

categories.name: Social Innovation & Entrepreneurship
categories.blurb: Ventures and models built to solve social problems at their root, not just their symptoms.

categories.name: Storytelling for Social Change
categories.blurb: How narrative and representation shape what feels possible.

categories.name: Imagination, Play & Creativity
categories.blurb: The raw material of possibility -- and how to keep it alive.

categories.name: Hope & Empowerment
categories.blurb: What it takes to believe change is possible, and act like it.

categories.name: Relationality, Connection & Community Health
categories.blurb: The condition of our shared civic and social life, and our capacity to act and belong together.

categories.name: Individual Healing Practice & Applied Somatics
categories.blurb: Embodied practices for processing what the mind alone can't resolve.

categories.name: Organizational Wellbeing & Embodied Leadership
categories.blurb: Leading and working in ways that don't extract from the people doing the work.

categories.name: Global Mental Health Research
categories.blurb: The evidence base on mental health and wellbeing, from clinical research to global policy.

categories.name: Intergenerational Trauma & Trauma-Informed Practice
categories.blurb: How harm travels across generations and systems -- and what trauma-informed practice does about it.

categories.name: Art & Healing
categories.blurb: Ritual, experience, and creative practice as sites of repair.

categories.name: Gen Z, Youth Experience & Identity
categories.blurb: What younger generations are actually experiencing, in their own words and data.

categories.name: Social Media, Attention Economy & Youth MH
categories.blurb: How platforms designed for attention are reshaping young people's minds and relationships.

categories.name: Tech, AI & Social Change
categories.blurb: Where technology, especially AI, is reshaping relationships, work, and society.

categories.name: Economy, Philanthropy & Wellbeing
categories.blurb: How money moves -- and what it would take for the economy and philanthropy to serve wellbeing rather than the reverse.

categories.name: Spiritual Ecology & Ecological Belonging
categories.blurb: Reweaving the human relationship to nature as kinship, not resource.

categories.name: Climate Justice & Eco-Anxiety
categories.blurb: The uneven human toll of the climate crisis, and what it does to our sense of the future.

categories.name: Government, Democracy & Polarization
categories.blurb: The health of democratic institutions and civic trust in a polarized moment.

categories.name: Racial Equity, Reproductive Justice & Gender
categories.blurb: Structural inequity across race, reproductive rights, and gender -- and the movements confronting it.
